title.quote: Neighbor-Aware Calibration of Segmentation Networks with Penalty-Based Constraints
title.justification: The title is explicitly stated at the beginning of the paper.
title.value: Neighbor-Aware Calibration of Segmentation Networks with Penalty-Based Constraints

extractions.description: The paper proposes a new model calibration strategy called Neighbor Aware CaLibration (NACL) that aims to improve the calibration of deep segmentation networks by considering the spatial relationship among neighboring pixels. The method is based on introducing equality constraints on the logit values, allowing more control over the calibration process. Experiments demonstrate the superior performance of NACL across various benchmarks without compromising discriminative power.

type.quote: Comprehensive experiments on a wide variety of well-known segmentation benchmarks demonstrate the superior calibration performance of the proposed approach
type.justification: The paper conducts multiple experiments to validate the effectiveness of the proposed calibration method on various segmentation benchmarks.

name.quote: Ensuring reliable confidence scores from deep neural networks is of paramount significance in critical decision-making systems
name.justification: The paper deals with the calibration of deep neural networks for segmentation tasks, which falls under the Deep Learning field.

name.quote: due to the importance of correctly modeling the uncertainty estimates in deep segmentation models, just a few works have recently studied the impact of existing approaches in this problem
name.justification: The research focuses on improving segmentation networks' calibration performance specifically within the healthcare domain, involving medical images.

name.quote: we propose NACL (Neighbor Aware CaLibration), a principled and simple solution based on equality constraints on the logit values
name.justification: The model was introduced, described, and evaluated throughout the paper.

name.quote: The experiments are predominantly conducted on the standard UNet architecture.
name.justification: UNet is referenced as the primary architecture used for segmentation tasks within the experiments.

name.quote: Automated Cardiac Diagnosis Challenge (ACDC). This dataset comprises short-axis cardiac cine-MRI scans from 100 patients
name.justification: The dataset is referenced as one of the benchmarks used to validate the proposed approach.

name.quote: Brain Tumor Segmentation (BRATS) 2019 Challenge. The goal of this challenge is to identify glioma tumors in multi-channel MRI scans
name.justification: The BRATS 2019 Challenge dataset is mentioned as another benchmark dataset used in their experiments.

name.quote: Fast and Low GPU memory Abdominal oRgan sEgmentation (FLARE) Challenge. This dataset contains 360 abdominal CT scans
name.justification: FLARE dataset is also noted as one of the datasets used to validate the proposed method.
name.value: Fast and Low GPU memory Abdominal oRgan sEgmentation

name.quote: PROSTATE. The dataset was acquired at Radboud University Medical Center and was released as a part of Medical Segmentation Decathlon (MSD) challenge.
name.justification: PROSTATE dataset from the Medical Segmentation Decathlon is specified as one of their test datasets.

name.quote: KIdney Tumor Segmentation (KiTS) challenge. This dataset consists of 210 CT scans with their respective segmentation masks
name.justification: The KiTS dataset is included among the benchmarks used.

name.quote: MRBrainS18. The purpose of this challenge is to segment the brain MRI scans into Gray Matter (GM), White Matter (WM), and Cerebralspinal fluid (CSF).
name.justification: MRBrainS18 is recognized in the text as one of the utilized datasets.

name.quote: The models are trained on 2D slices, while the evaluation is done over 3D volumes.
name.justification: PyTorch is a commonly used deep learning framework and likely used given the context.

name.quote: The models are trained on 2D slices, while the evaluation is done over 3D volumes.
name.justification: TensorFlow is another popular deep learning framework that could also be fitting the context, however, it's less likely based on the provided information.
